findings.title: Fixture upload input reaches report renderer
findings.trust_boundary: HTTP request body crosses into report rendering
findings.root_cause: Fixture handler passes request-controlled body into a privileged rendering boundary without a validation decision point.
findings.evidence: Synthetic fixture code shows direct local data flow from handle_upload to render_report.
findings.impact: The chain can affect trusted report rendering decisions in the fixture scenario.
findings.minimal_remediation: Add a local validation boundary before report rendering and test rejected fixture inputs.
findings.regression_test_idea: Use benign marker strings and mocked request objects to assert untrusted input is treated as data.
findings.issue_title: [Security][Critical] Fixture upload input reaches report renderer

assessment_notes.bug_existence: Direct fixture data flow is present.
assessment_notes.attacker_reachability: Route wiring is synthetic and requires human review before production claims.
assessment_notes.boundary_crossing: Untrusted request body reaches trusted renderer boundary.

findings.title: Fixture token trust boundary is implicit
findings.trust_boundary: request header token to rendering authorization decision
findings.root_cause: Fixture token is forwarded without an explicit local authorization check.
findings.evidence: Synthetic fixture shows header value forwarded into trusted report rendering context.
findings.impact: May combine with SEC-101 as a defensive chain requiring local validation.
findings.minimal_remediation: Validate fixture token before using it in trusted rendering context.
findings.regression_test_idea: Mock request headers and assert missing token takes a safe local path.
findings.issue_title: [Security][High] Fixture token trust boundary is implicit

assessment_notes.bug_existence: Implicit token trust is visible in fixture code.
assessment_notes.boundary_crossing: Request header crosses into trusted renderer context.
assessment_notes.impact_assessment: Impact depends on renderer policy not modeled by the fixture.

findings.title: Fixture logging path needs coverage follow-up
findings.trust_boundary: rendered report to local audit log
findings.source_to_sink: render_report return value -> downstream logging not included in fixture
findings.root_cause: Fixture intentionally leaves downstream logging out of scope.
findings.evidence: No weaponized payload or external service is present; this is a coverage follow-up item.
findings.impact: Potential logging confusion only if downstream logging exists.
findings.minimal_remediation: Add coverage for downstream logging before elevating severity.
findings.regression_test_idea: Extend fixture with benign log sink and assert encoded marker handling.
findings.issue_title: [Security][Medium] Fixture logging path needs coverage follow-up

assessment_notes.bug_existence: Fixture intentionally does not prove this path.
assessment_notes.attacker_reachability: Not assessed in this workflow fixture.
